alternatives_rejected.alternative: Approve the original design and fix violations post-implementation
alternatives_rejected.rejection_reason: Violations are architectural, not cosmetic. Fixing post-implementation would require rewriting the artifact schema, the source reader, and the registration path — equivalent effort to redesigning upfront with higher risk of compounding drift.

alternatives_rejected.alternative: Block SKL entirely until all constraints are met
alternatives_rejected.rejection_reason: The correct response to design violations is revision, not abandonment. The judgment capture path (SKL-J) has no dependencies on the violated components and can start immediately.

assumptions.assumption: The shared contract module is the canonical path for all new artifact types.
assumptions.falsification_signal: An artifact type is accepted into the pipeline without a corresponding schema file in the contract module.

assumptions.assumption: AgentExecutionTrace artifacts contain sufficient structured data to extract a reusable pattern.
assumptions.falsification_signal: EVAL-SKL-001 fails because trace artifacts lack the fields needed for pattern extraction.

claims_considered.claim_text: New artifact schemas must go through the shared contract module — not be introduced as side effects of feature work.
Any autonomy increase requires explicit policy constraints and eval coverage before enablement — not just a human in the loop.
SKL reading PR descriptions violates artifact-first — the correct source is AgentExecutionTrace artifacts from the data-lake.
A plain JSON registry updated by PR merge is not mediated by the control layer — registration must produce a PromotionRecordArtifact.
SKL is explicitly gated on all four cleanup PRs merging AND the shared contract module being GOV-10 certified.

expected_negative: SKL trace extraction is delayed until five pre-conditions are met
Additional upfront work required: schema PR, policy artifact, two eval cases
expected_positive: SKL design is compliant with governing constraints before any code is written
Red team findings are captured as a permanent record preventing re-litigation
The revised design has a clear implementation sequence with explicit hard pre-conditions
consequences.monitoring_plan: Verify all five SKL pre-conditions are met before any SKL trace extraction work begins. The schema PR gate is the primary enforcement mechanism.

open_questions: Whether AgentExecutionTrace artifacts currently emitted by Claude Code sessions contain sufficient structure for SKL pattern extraction — requires EVAL-SKL-001 to determine.
question_under_judgment: Is the original SKL Skillify Loop design safe to implement as specified?
rationale: The original SKL design introduced a new artifact schema outside the shared contract module, assumed PR description prose as a valid artifact source, proposed a plain JSON registry with no control layer mediation, and treated human approval as sufficient mitigation for an autonomy increase without requiring policy constraints or eval coverage. Each of these violated a load-bearing design constraint. The design was revised before implementation — not blocked — because the core insight (skills as governed artifacts with provenance) is sound. Only the implementation path violated constraints.
rules_applied: SSC-DESIGN-001 section 12: every change must make the system safer, more measurable, or more trustworthy
artifact-first: JSON is canonical, prose is not a valid artifact source
fail-closed: ambiguous scope halts to a finding
autonomy-expansion-gate: autonomy increases require policy constraints and eval coverage
